behaviors.description: Review input is scoped to changed lines only.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: diff extraction -> review-input builder. The behavior text does not require real PR API/OS/subprocess, and correctness does not depend on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real diff-parsing and prompt/input-construction modules together using a fixture diff containing both changed and unchanged file regions; assert the emitted review payload includes only changed-line snippets and excludes unchanged content.

behaviors.description: Inline comments attach to correct diff positions on the PR.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets positions in the PR diff view, which is enforced by real GitHub API semantics/rendering. Mocks can hide position/commit-side/path mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments with computed position metadata and then read back review comments from GitHub; assert each comment lands on the intended file/hunk/line in the live diff.

behaviors.description: Agent finds at least one real defect in a PR with a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real model judgment quality (detecting an actual bug), not just orchestration. Deterministic stubs would always hide this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live analysis model on a seeded PR diff containing a known bug and assert at least one finding correctly flags that defect class/location (allowing non-deterministic retries or success-rate threshold).

behaviors.description: Each summary finding maps to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state consistency (inline-post results -> summary assembler). Real OS/network/model output is not required to expose ID-linking bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute posting + summary-assembly pipeline with a deterministic finding set and stubbed comment-post responses returning unique IDs; assert every summary entry references an existing ID produced in that exact run context.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped before final summary payload posting.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in local orchestration/aggregation across modules (collector -> serializer -> summary payload). No explicit real infra or model-quality dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run inline-finding collection through summary-payload builder with multiple findings (including edge cases like duplicate paths/severities) and assert payload cardinality and identity coverage match the full inline set.

behaviors.description: Read diff -> post inline comments -> post summary succeeds on a real PR with fixed precomputed findings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real pull request and live sequence over external API calls. Precomputed findings remove model dependence, so Agentic is disqualified by text.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end against a real test PR using a fixed findings fixture; verify diff read succeeds, all inline comments are created, and top-level summary posts with expected references/status.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level rejected: Agentic not required because behavior says "fixed precomputed finding set."

behaviors.description: Full live run with model-generated findings completes on a real PR without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Includes real PR infrastructure plus correctness of model-generated findings path. Minimum level must include real LLM behavior; single model-analysis stage is stated, so Workflow is not strictly required by text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full autonomous run on a real test PR (read diff, live model analysis, inline posting, summary posting); assert run finishes unattended and artifacts are internally consistent (posted inline comments and summary linkage).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level rejected: Workflow not mandated because behavior text specifies one model-generation phase, not multiple agentic decision stages.
